focus: security: path-traversal & state-dir resolution, command injection in spawned codex/opencode

title: Same-kind fanout replicas still share real CLI homes and lockfiles
detail: fanout-run creates per-lineage .executor-state directories and passes SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR, but its own comment says native CLIs read CODEX_HOME, OPENCODE_HOME, or Claude home envs instead. buildExecutorDispatchEnv preserves parent CODEX_, OPENCODE_, and CLAUDE_ environment keys, so same-kind replicas can still share real CLI state and lockfiles under normal count>1 fanout. The tests assert distinct SPECKIT_CODEX_STATE_DIR values, not distinct effective CLI homes.
claim: Same-kind cli-codex, cli-opencode, or cli-claude-code fanout replicas are not effectively state-isolated because the script sets only SPECKIT hints while preserving the real CLI home env vars the children use.
counterevidenceSought: Checked the lineage label schema, state-dir assignment, child environment filtering, and same-kind replica tests. Labels are directory-safe and SPECKIT state dirs are unique, but no inspected code changes the effective CLI homes or serializes same-kind replicas when those homes remain shared.
alternativeExplanation: If current supported CLI builds actually honor SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR for lockfiles and session state despite the in-code comment, then the isolation works and the defect collapses to stale documentation/tests.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if integration evidence shows codex/opencode/claude use the SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR variables for their lockfiles, or if production fanout prevents concurrent same-kind replicas.
suggestedFix: Either give each spawned CLI an effective per-lineage home/state root that the CLI actually honors, or serialize same-kind replicas behind a per-kind lock when home relocation is not safe. Add a regression that checks effective CODEX_HOME/OPENCODE_HOME/Claude home isolation or proves same-kind concurrent dispatch is blocked.

title: baseArtifactDir can relocate fanout artifacts outside the canonical review tree
detail: baseArtifactDir rejects only literal .. segments. Absolute paths without .. pass, and the script derives lineagesDir, ledger, summary, checkpoint, state dirs, and log writes from that value. buildLoopPrompt then tells child agents to write all outputs to the resulting lineage directory, while the opencode branch runs with --dangerously-skip-permissions. A malformed or injected base artifact path can move fanout state and child writes outside the expected packet/artifact root.
claim: A baseArtifactDir such as /tmp/outside-root passes validation and becomes the root for fanout state plus child-agent output instructions, so the script lacks a containment guard for artifact root resolution.
counterevidenceSought: Checked for a later path.resolve/path.relative containment check, label-based escape, and shell interpolation. Labels are safe and there is no shell injection, but no runtime guard keeps baseArtifactDir under a canonical artifact root after resolution.
alternativeExplanation: If baseArtifactDir is a trusted host-only parameter and arbitrary absolute artifact roots are intentionally supported, the code is behaving as designed, but that trust boundary is not enforced by the script.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if callers prove baseArtifactDir is never derived from user/configurable input and external absolute artifact roots are documented as supported for all spawned CLI modes.
suggestedFix: Resolve baseArtifactDir to an absolute path and require it to stay under the canonical artifact root for the resolved spec folder or another explicit allowlisted root. Reject null bytes, traversal, absolute escapes, and symlink escapes before creating directories or spawning child CLIs.

title: cli-claude-code configDir accepts arbitrary traversal/absolute paths into CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR
detail: configDir is validated only as a non-empty string. fanout-run expands ~ and ~/ but otherwise passes the value unchanged into CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR. A fanout config such as ../../somewhere or /tmp/attacker-claude passes validation and steers the spawned Claude process at an arbitrary config root, which can switch credentials/account state or load attacker-controlled config.
claim: A cli-claude-code lineage can supply a traversal or absolute configDir that is exported directly as CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR for the child process without containment checks.
counterevidenceSought: Checked executor schema, configDir tests, and fanout-run env construction. configDir is restricted to cli-claude-code and passed via environment rather than shell, but I found no traversal, absolute-path, null-byte, or allowlist validation.
alternativeExplanation: Arbitrary config roots may be an intentional operator escape hatch for multi-account testing; in that case this should be documented as trusted input rather than treated as a safe fanout config field.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if fanout configs are explicitly trusted operator-only inputs and arbitrary Claude config roots are a documented supported feature.
suggestedFix: Restrict configDir to a safe home-relative account directory pattern, or resolve it and require it to stay inside HOME or an explicit allowlist. Reject .., null bytes, and absolute escapes. Add negative tests for traversal and absolute paths.
